response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a highly experienced full-stack and distributed-systems engineer, but the role demands deep, sustained C++ expertise as its core requirement. His C++ exposure is limited to a six-month stint at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project; it does not appear in his skills list at all. The role additionally requires industrial-cybersecurity knowledge (IEC 62443, SDL), MQTT and industrial-protocol experience (Modbus, OPC UA, DNP 3.0), and an industrial-automation domain background — none of which the candidate demonstrates. His strengths in TypeScript, Node.js, and event-driven web architectures are largely orthogonal to the position's requirements.
strengths: Extensive experience with event-driven and distributed architectures, including a high-throughput micro-services platform handling millions of requests per second at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby
Strong Node.js and REST API background, which partially aligns with the role's advantageous web-technology skills
Demonstrated software-craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, mentoring/workshops) that maps to the role's quality and collaboration expectations
concerns: C++ proficiency is effectively absent from the candidate's active skillset; the only relevant exposure was a six-month junior role in 2014–2015 and a thesis project
No evidence of industrial-cybersecurity knowledge, Secure Development Lifecycle practices, or IEC 62443 compliance work
No experience with industrial-automation domains, MQTT, or industrial protocols (Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0)
